action.text:
Yes, that’s it.
Loading can start when all necessary terminal and tanker valves in the loading system are open, and the ship has informed the terminal that she is ready. Loading starts at a slow rate to a single tank. The shore pumps are started only after the ship has informed the terminal that she is receiving cargo in the correct tank.
A crew member is standing at the manifolds, in case there is a leaking connection or any other problem. He is then ready to instruct the shore to stop loading.
The valves on board the ship are operated manually from the deck or automatically from the cargo control room.
Would you like to visit the cargo control room? Fine, click on the NEXT button to see the cargo control room.
